boundary_payload.workflow_description: AI-assisted claims intake, triage, and initial assessment for insurance carrier submissions.
boundary_notes: This boundary covers the AI-assisted claims intake path only.
Manual review and human-only processing paths are excluded.
Coverage is scoped to the declared entrypoints.

questions.prompt: Does the system use AI/ML in its decision-making or operations?
questions.notes: The proof pack contains receipts from an AI workflow execution, confirming AI is in use.

questions.prompt: Are AI model calls documented with inputs, outputs, and timing?
questions.notes: Receipt count confirms model call receipts exist. Each receipt records arguments, responses, latency, and model identity in receipt_pack.jsonl.

questions.prompt: Is there a count of AI call sites and their instrumentation coverage?
Is there a governance framework for AI oversight?
questions.notes: Governance framework existence is an organizational fact. Assay can evidence runtime controls but not board-level governance structure.

questions.prompt: Are runtime controls applied to AI decisions (guardrails, validators, policy checks)?
questions.notes: Claim verification confirms runtime controls (guardian verdicts, policy checks) were applied and their outcomes recorded.

questions.prompt: Is there an audit trail for AI decisions that an independent reviewer can verify?
questions.notes: The proof pack is a signed, independently verifiable audit trail. Integrity and claims are checked offline by anyone.

questions.prompt: Can the evidence be verified without access to the vendor's systems?
questions.notes: Proof packs are offline-verifiable by design: Ed25519 signature, SHA-256 hashes, no server dependency.

questions.prompt: Is tamper detection in place for AI evidence and audit records?
questions.notes: Tamper detection is the core Assay property: JCS canonicalization, SHA-256 hashes, Ed25519 signatures. One changed byte breaks verification.

questions.prompt: Is the model identity recorded for each AI call?
questions.notes: Each model call receipt in receipt_pack.jsonl includes model identity, version, and provider. Receipt count confirms calls were recorded.
Risk classification is an organizational judgment. Assay records which models were used but does not classify their risk level.

questions.prompt: Is there evidence of model validation or testing?
questions.notes: Claim checks in the proof pack attest whether declared behavioral standards passed or failed at runtime.

questions.scope: Out of scope for runtime evidence
questions.out_of_scope_note: Training data provenance is a pre-deployment concern. Assay covers post-deployment runtime evidence, not training pipelines.

questions.prompt: Are data retention and deletion policies in place for AI inputs/outputs?
questions.notes: Data retention is organizational policy. Assay receipts record what was processed but do not enforce retention schedules.

questions.prompt: Does this evidence packet establish legal or regulatory compliance?
questions.out_of_scope_note: This packet provides tamper-evident execution evidence, not legal conclusions. Compliance determination requires legal counsel and regulatory review.
